title: Professional Resume — Sarah Chen
description: A software engineer's resume with personal contact details, education history, and employment information.
originalText:
SARAH CHEN
Senior Software Engineer

123 Maple Avenue, Apt 4B
San Francisco, CA 94107

Email: sarah.chen@gmail.com
Phone: (415) 555-0192
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/sarachen

PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY
Experienced software engineer with 8+ years building distributed systems. Previously led the backend team at Meridian Technologies, where I reported to Dr. Smith. Known for delivering scalable microservices and mentoring junior developers.

EXPERIENCE

Senior Software Engineer — Meridian Technologies
January 2019 – Present
- Architected a real-time data pipeline processing 2M events/day
- Managed a team of 6 engineers across San Francisco and Austin offices
- Reduced infrastructure costs by 34% through containerization

Software Engineer — Bloom Health Inc.
March 2015 – December 2018
- Built HIPAA-compliant patient portal serving 50,000+ users
- Integrated with insurance provider APIs (Aetna, Blue Cross)
- SSN verification module for identity confirmation (SSN: 478-93-2156)

EDUCATION
B.S. Computer Science — Stanford University, 2014
Date of Birth: March 15, 1992

REFERENCES
Available upon request. Contact Sarah Chen at sarah.chen@gmail.com or call (415) 555-0192.

Generated from IP: 192.168.1.42

reasoning.summary: This is a full personal name appearing as the document header, strongly indicating it is the document subject's real identity.
reasoning.possibleRisk: Full name can be combined with other details in this resume (address, phone, SSN) to fully identify and impersonate this individual.

spans.text:
123 Maple Avenue, Apt 4B
San Francisco, CA 94107

reasoning.summary: This is a complete residential address spanning two lines, including street, apartment number, city, state, and ZIP code.
reasoning.possibleRisk: A full residential address enables physical tracking, unwanted contact, or stalking when combined with the person's name.

reasoning.summary: This is a personal email address in standard format, directly tied to the document subject by name.
reasoning.possibleRisk: Email addresses enable unsolicited contact, phishing attacks, and can be used to look up other accounts and personal data online.

reasoning.summary: This is a US phone number in standard parenthetical area code format, listed in the contact section of the resume.
reasoning.possibleRisk: Phone numbers enable unwanted calls, SMS phishing, SIM swapping attacks, and can be used to verify identity on other platforms.

reasoning.summary: This appears to be a company name. While it could indirectly identify the document subject through employment history, organization names are generally not classified as direct PII and fall below the redaction threshold.
reasoning.possibleRisk: Combined with a job title and date range, an employer name could narrow down identity to a very small group of people.

reasoning.summary: This looks like a person's name due to the 'Dr.' title, but 'Smith' is extremely common and appears here as a passing reference to a supervisor, not as a primary subject of the document. Kept visible as a near-miss.
reasoning.possibleRisk: Minimal direct risk — 'Dr. Smith' without a first name, institution, or specialty is too generic to identify anyone specific.

reasoning.summary: This is a company name in the employment section. Like Meridian Technologies, it is an organizational identifier rather than direct PII, though it contributes to a profile that could narrow down the person's identity.
reasoning.possibleRisk: Employment at a health company combined with the date range and other resume details could significantly narrow identity.

reasoning.summary: This matches the pattern of a US Social Security Number (XXX-XX-XXXX) and appears in the context of identity verification. SSNs are among the highest-risk PII categories.
reasoning.possibleRisk: A Social Security Number is the single most dangerous piece of PII to expose — it enables identity theft, fraudulent credit applications, tax fraud, and is nearly impossible to change once compromised.

reasoning.summary: This date appears immediately after a 'Date of Birth:' label, confirming it is the document subject's birthdate rather than an arbitrary date.
signals: Plausible birth year for the education timeline
reasoning.possibleRisk: Date of birth is a key identity verification factor used by banks, insurers, and government agencies — combined with name and SSN, it completes the identity theft trifecta.

reasoning.summary: This is a second occurrence of the document subject's full name, appearing in the references section. Same person as the header name.
reasoning.possibleRisk: Full name can be combined with other details in this resume (address, phone, SSN) to fully identify and impersonate this individual.

reasoning.summary: This is the second occurrence of the document subject's personal email address, appearing in the references section.
reasoning.possibleRisk: Email addresses enable unsolicited contact, phishing attacks, and can be used to look up other accounts and personal data online.

reasoning.summary: This is the second occurrence of the document subject's phone number, appearing in the references section.
reasoning.possibleRisk: Phone numbers enable unwanted calls, SMS phishing, SIM swapping attacks, and can be used to verify identity on other platforms.

reasoning.summary: This matches an IPv4 address pattern, but 192.168.x.x is a private/local network range that cannot identify a person on the public internet. Confidence is unavailable because the detection was pattern-only with no contextual confirmation.
reasoning.possibleRisk: Private IP addresses pose virtually no identification risk on their own, unlike public IPs which can approximate geographic location.
